key_insight: Bulk Cs2AgBiI6, long considered synthetically inaccessible, can be isolated in phase-pure microcrystal and thin-film forms by carefully controlling moisture and using halide exchange from Cs2AgBiBr6.
engineering_relevance: This enables a lead-free iodide double-perovskite absorber platform with visible-light absorption and measurable photoconductive response for optoelectronic device development.
method: Prepared phase-pure Cs2AgBiBr6 microcrystals as a precursor
Removed trace moisture to enable complete Br→I anion exchange and isolate Cs2AgBiI6 microcrystals
Made phase-pure Cs2AgBiI6 films by flash thermal evaporation followed by anion exchange
evidence_signal: The resulting Cs2AgBiI6 showed a 1.70 ± 0.05 eV optical bandgap, near-infrared photoluminescence at 1.03 eV, long-term room-temperature stability, and photoconductivity in films.
watch_item: The synthesis appears highly sensitive to residual moisture, so reproducibility and scale-up may require strict inert, dry processing.
paper_title: Synthesis of the Elusive Bulk Iodide Double-Perovskite Semiconductor, Cs2AgBiI6: Microcrystals and Photoconductive Films
